jorgensen-v-jml-rose-pty-ltd-security-fo.lesson: Repeat AI-citation offenders invite security-for-costs orders; flag prior hallucination history when opposing pro-se litigants.

application-by-pennisi-2026-02-05.lesson: Fabricated authorities cannot establish exceptional circumstances; verify every cited case against primary sources.

applicant-v-bc-college-of-physicians-and.lesson: Always run cite-checks in CanLII before filing; unlocatable authorities undermine the entire submission.

rsr-road-surface-recycling-v-bonnechere-.lesson: If counsel cannot produce the quoted passage on demand, the quote is AI-hallucinated; verify quotations against the source decision pre-filing.

pasuengos-v-minister-for-immigration-and.lesson: Never cite from AI-generated Google summaries; treat any Overview snippet as unverified until confirmed in primary databases.

ortiz-fatima-cecilia-v-bookingcom-y-otro.lesson: Attach verifiable copies and full citations (sala, jurisdiction, date) for every precedent; generic AI-style cites trigger sanctions and bar referral.

broadwater-tower-2026-02-02.lesson: AI routinely invents statutory content; read the actual section text before citing any provision.

health-care-complaints-commission-v-camp.lesson: AI hallucinates both case names and paragraph numbers; verify pinpoint cites, not just the case reference.

psahsc-v-tchampet-2026-01-30.lesson: Microsoft Co-Pilot is not a legal research tool; ban consumer chatbots for drafting submissions and quote-pull every authority.

rasko-v-icbc-2026-01-30.lesson: Opposing parties now routinely flag AI hallucinations; expect adversarial cite-checks on every submission.

roux-v-van-greunen-2026-01-29.lesson: A 'compiled' Word document of a judgment is a red flag; courts expect neutral citations traceable to official reporters.

howse-v-coulton-2026-01-29.lesson: Jurisdictional arguments built on hallucinated appellate authority collapse on verification; cite-check before relying on procedural case law.

folarin-v-the-immigration-services-commi.lesson: ChatGPT confidently cites cases that do not exist across multiple jurisdictions; never use it for legal research without primary-source verification.

sas-la-volumerie-2026-01-28.lesson: Courts are dismissing filings that read as AI-drafted without specifics; a human lawyer must substantively edit every AI-assisted pleading.

davidson-v-pcl-constructors-inc-2026-01-.lesson: Opposing counsel should flag suspected AI fabrications on the record; it shifts the burden and preserves cost arguments.

cohen-v-egged-transportation-ltd-2026-01.lesson: Courts are ordering amendments rather than dismissals for pro-se AI errors; attorneys will not get the same leniency.

gummadi-usha-rani-v-sure-mallikarjuna-ra.lesson: Judges using AI to draft orders is now appellate-reversible error; never let AI generate reasoning or authority in judicial work product.

tarek-jabarin-v-national-insurance-insti.lesson: Even small monetary penalties now attach to fake AI citations; a single fabricated case is enough to draw a fine.
